title: [WIP]test c/common config pkg
body:
- Vendor in c/common config pkg for containers.conf https://github.com/containers/common/pull/20
- Set OCIRunime if runtime from c/common config is empty
- Vendor in [branch](https://github.com/containers/buildah/compare/v1.11.5...QiWang19:1.11.5_unshare_c/common) from build replace buildah/unshare with common/pkg/unshare to avoid unshare redefined both in buidlah&common under vendor folder error
- Remove libpod/pkg/config

Signed-off-by: Qi Wang <qiwan@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *QiWang19* To complete the [pull request process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process), please assign **tomsweeneyredhat** You can assign the PR to them by writing `/assign @tomsweeneyredhat` in a comment when ready. The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@baude @mheon PTAL

config management will now be in c/common
Changes LGTM, but we may want to hold until after the next release.
Agree - I don't want this landing before a 1.7.x

This will not go in until 1.8 most likely.  It needs to get merged into Buildah fist to make sure Buildah and Podman do the correct thing on podman build.

Hopefully we can roll forward with this in Buildah soon.  Although I am not sure if everything works correctly without a config file, and that the config files merge properly yet.

We want the default to be everything commented out if possible, and then only have /usr/share/containers/containers.conf has specific settings for a distribution.

/usr/share/containers/containers.conf, /etc/containers/containers.conf and ~/.config/containers/containers.conf need to be able to be merged correctly for this to fully work.

Also containers.conf format has to be fully agreed upon before we release.
